name: Trail of the Valleys
name_original: Sendero de los Valles (PRC-BU 168) — circular desde Vadocondes
description: Hike the moderate 11.4-mile Sendero de los Valles (PRC-BU 168) circular route from Vadocondes, exploring riverside forests and vineyards.
region_intro: Hiking around Vadocondes offers diverse landscapes in the heart of the Ribera del Duero region. The area features a predominantly flat relief, with altitudes ranging from 800 to 930 meters, shaped by the Duero River and its surroundings. Trails often wind through expansive vineyards and riverside forests, reflecting the region's winemaking tradition. Nearby geological formations include impressive gorges and cliffs, providing varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Vadocondes The most popular hiking route is Sendero de las Viñas (PRC-BU 71) — circular desde Quemada por Zazuar, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through vineyards, offering an immersive agricultural experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trail of the Valleys, a moderate 11.5 miles (18.4 km) path. This route guides hikers through riverside forests and vineyards, showcasing the natural beauty of the Duero River valley. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Andaluz y Iglesia de Nuestra Señora – circular por el Camino del Cid desde Castilllejo de Robledo, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through medieval landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Vadocondes is defined by the Duero River, extensive vineyards, and riverside forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Vadocondes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 20 reviews. Nearly 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Vadocondes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vadocondes?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in and around Vadocondes, offering a wide variety of options for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Vadocondes?
A: Hiking in Vadocondes offers a blend of picturesque landscapes. You'll find trails winding through extensive vineyards, characteristic of the Ribera del Duero wine region, and serene riverside forests along the Duero River. The area also features a predominantly flat relief, with nearby dramatic geological formations like gorges and cliffs, such as the Hoces del Río Riaza.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vadocondes?
A: The best time for hiking in Vadocondes is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is mild and pleasant. Spring brings blooming vineyards and lush riverside vegetation, while autumn offers beautiful colors and the grape harvest season. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly, though hiking is possible year-round.

Q: Are there hiking options for different fitness levels in Vadocondes?
A: Yes, Vadocondes caters to various fitness levels. You'll find a good selection of easy paths, perfect for a leisurely stroll, as well as moderate routes that offer a bit more challenge. For experienced hikers, there are also a few more demanding trails available.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Vadocondes area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Vadocondes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Notable circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131558264/sendero-de-las-vinas-prc-bu-71-circular-desde-quemada-por-zazuar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de las Viñas (PRC-BU 71)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through vineyards, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131577216/trail-of-the-valleys" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Valleys&lt;/a&gt;, exploring riverside forests and vineyards.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Vadocondes?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Vadocondes area. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in nature reserves like the Hoces del Río Riaza, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails suitable for children?
A: Yes, there are several easy hiking trails around Vadocondes that are suitable for families with children. These routes often feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037974590/hermitage-of-the-most-holy-trinity-from-fuentespina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Most Holy Trinity from Fuentespina&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.2 km path.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature natural landmarks like gorges or cliffs?
A: Absolutely. While Vadocondes itself is characterized by river valleys and vineyards, nearby natural attractions include impressive geological formations. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of the Hoces del Río Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the river, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2557040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct of the Madrid–Burgos Railway over the Riaza Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable sight in the area.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore on a hike?
A: Vadocondes is rich in history and culture. You can hike portions of the historic Camino del Cid, which offers glimpses into medieval landscapes. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063970015/castillo-de-andaluz-y-iglesia-de-nuestra-senora-circular-por-el-camino-del-cid-desde-castilllejo-de-robledo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Andaluz y Iglesia de Nuestra Señora – circular por el Camino del Cid desde Castilllejo de Robledo&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5030883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo de Robledo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3395685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Vid&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Vadocondes?
A: While specific trailhead parking information varies by route, Vadocondes and its surrounding villages generally offer public parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen trail for the most convenient parking options, often found near town centers or recreational areas.

Q: How can I reach the hiking trails around Vadocondes using public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach specific trailheads directly might be limited in some rural areas around Vadocondes. However, regional bus services connect Vadocondes with larger towns like Aranda de Duero, from where you might find local connections or taxis to reach more remote starting points. Planning ahead is recommended.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Vadocondes?
A: The hiking trails in Vadocondes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the immersive experience of walking through the vineyards and riverside forests, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to combine outdoor activity with the region's rich winemaking culture.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, there are longer and more challenging routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040344144/senda-del-suero-from-la-vid-circular-via-langa-de-duero-and-its-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Suero from La Vid&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult circular hike that spans over 23 kilometers and passes through Langa de Duero and its castle, offering a full day's adventure.
tips: Town of Burgos located in the region of La Ribera. Deposits have been found dating to the inhabitants of this site from the 7th century BC. C. Although the entire town was classified as an Asset of Cultural Interest, its two bridges over the Duero river (one from the 18th century and the other from the 19th century) and the Church of the Assumption stand out.
Town of Burgos that owes its name to the small adjacent hill, on which we find a cross with a viewpoint of the town and the surrounding lands (it is worth going up to enjoy the views).

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Sendero de los Valles (PRC-BU 168)?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Vadocondes. You can typically find parking within the village, especially near the Vadocondes Historic Center, which is a good starting point for the hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or experienced hikers?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this trail as 'moderate'. While some sources vary in their difficulty assessment, describing it from 'medium' to 'high', its 18.4 km distance and approximately 200 meters of elevation gain make it a good challenge for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness. It's not overly technical but requires stamina.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start along a lush riverside forest by the Duero River, then transition to vineyards and agricultural fields. The path ascends to an elevated plain, includes a comfortable section known as the Camino de la Vid, and crosses moorland areas with ravines. Expect a mix of forest paths, dirt tracks, and possibly some paved sections through villages.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, from dense riparian forests with poplars, aspens, and willows along the Duero River, to extensive vineyards and cereal fields. You'll pass through areas with holm oaks and junipers, and enjoy panoramic views of the impressive Cuerno Blanco mountain, the Duero river valley, and La Calabaza mountain, which is covered by pine and holm oak forests.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The riverside environment is particularly rich in birdlife. Keep an eye out for common thrushes, common chiffchaffs, grey herons, ducks, and coots. The varied ecosystems along the route provide habitats for a range of other local fauna as well.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail ascends to higher ground, you'll be treated to impressive views. Look for vistas of the Cuerno Blanco mountain from the elevated plain, and later, scenic panoramas of the Duero river valley and La Calabaza mountain from the moorland areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural landscapes, the trail passes through the charming village of Vadocondes, with its Vadocondes Historic Center, and also takes you through Santa Cruz de la Salceda. The route itself is notable for its ecological diversity, linking riverside ecosystems with Mediterranean forests and agricultural lands.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero de los Valles (PRC-BU 168) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. Given its natural setting and varied terrain, it's a great option for hiking with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and respect local regulations regarding leashes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is accessible year-round, offering different charms with each season. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, while summer can be warm but enjoyable, especially along the shaded riverside sections. Winter offers a quieter experience, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Sendero de los Valles (PRC-BU 168). It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the official 'Sendero de los Valles' (PRC-BU 168) for about 97% of its length. It also briefly intersects with sections of the 'Etapa 09: Langa de Duero – Aranda de Duero' and the 'Ruta del Duero'.
